name: Strong-CP problem
summary: QCD allows a CP-violating topological term theta * G G-tilde with a dimensionless coefficient theta that should naturally be O(1). Neutron-EDM bounds force |theta_eff| < ~10^-10. No SM mechanism enforces this smallness; theta receives independent contributions from the QCD vacuum angle and the quark-mass-matrix phase, with no symmetry tying them to cancel.

characteristic_numbers.note: Bound from |d_n| < 1.8e-26 e cm (nEDM 2020). Natural expectation in the absence of a protective symmetry is theta ~ O(1).

principles_stressed.note: Setting theta = 0 in the SM does not restore a symmetry once the quark mass matrix is complex; an axial U(1) rotates the phase between theta and the mass matrix without changing the physical theta_eff. Solving strong-CP requires either a new symmetry (PQ, parity, or CP), a special quark-mass structure (Nelson-Barr), or a vanishing up-quark mass (excluded by lattice).

history: QCD theta term recognized as physical after the U(1)_A puzzle was resolved by 't Hooft (1976). PQ axion proposed (Peccei-Wess 1977; Wilczek; Weinberg) as the dominant solution. Massless up quark (Kaplan-Manohar) ruled out by lattice ~2010. Nelson-Barr revived periodically. Parity-symmetric models (Babu-Mohapatra) remain alive.

references.citation: Peccei and Quinn, 'CP conservation in the presence of pseudoparticles', PRL 38, 1440 (1977)
't Hooft, 'Symmetry breaking through Bell-Jackiw anomalies', PRL 37, 8 (1976)
Abel et al. (nEDM), 'Measurement of the permanent electric dipole moment of the neutron', PRL 124, 081803 (2020)
